description: Study matmul_block helper API

error: TypeError: split_work_to_cores() does not accept CoreGrid, expects CoreCoord or CoreRangeSet. Returns tuple(int, CoreRangeSet, CoreRangeSet, CoreRangeSet, int, int)

description: split_work_to_cores returns (num_core_groups, crs1, crs2, crs_all, group1_size, group2_size). Need to use the returned CoreRangeSets for core_grid and iterate over cores using the group structure.
Find split_work_to_cores usage

error: matmul_block expects Buf& (CircularBuffer) not uint32_t CB IDs. Need to get CB objects from IDs.
Dispatch timeout / device hang on single_tile_32x32 shape. Compile passed, runtime hung.

description: CB sync mismatch: reader pushes partial blocks (1 tile for 32x32) but compute expects full B_q=4. Fix: use min(B_q_default, S_q_t) as compile-time args so reader/compute/writer all agree on block size.

TRISC0 stuck at Phase 4 CopyTile<cb_m>::wait_upfront (HeldBulk). compute_kernel_hw_startup called mid-kernel before reduce may be causing deadlock (MMIO write, unsafe mid-MAIN). Try removing it — reduce helper may handle its own reconfig internally.

error: 3rd hang at Phase 4 CopyTile<cb_m>::wait_upfront (HeldBulk). Tried: (1) removed mid-kernel compute_kernel_hw_startup, (2) reordered boot init to eltwise first. Both still hang at same location.

description: Analyze SDPA kernel structure

F1: Add cb_push_back after pack_tile in Phase 0 init and Q-block reinit. F2: Add tile_regs_commit/wait. F3: Remove cb_pop_front(cb_m_new) before copy. F4: Use OperandKind::Block for 1D tiles(B_q) chains.

error: PCC=0.124 on single_tile_32x32 (threshold 0.995). Hang fixed by F1-F4 fixes. Now need to debug numerical correctness.

description: Q tiles popped by first KV block matmul (WaitAndPopPerKBlock). Subsequent KV blocks have no Q data. Fix: use WaitAndRetainOnLastBlock for in0 (Q) in QK^T matmul.
All-ones input: output=[1,inf,inf,inf]. First element correct, rest inf. Likely l_i=0 → div by zero in normalize phase. Need DEVICE_PRINT to trace m_i, l_i, scores at each phase.
WaitAndRetainOnLastBlock for Q may not work correctly with num_k_blocks=1 — Q tiles read at wrong index. Simpler fix: use WaitAndPopPerKBlock for Q and have reader re-push Q per KV block.
l_i has value 31.375 only in col 0 (REDUCE_ROW output). DivBinary (SFPU) doesnt broadcast across cols. Fix: use recip(l_i) then BinaryFpu<Mul, Col> which broadcasts at FPU level.
multi_tile_128x64 hang: matmul_block InitMode::Short reconfig_data_format stuck on 2nd KV block after eltwise chains. Fix: use DataFormatReconfig::NONE since all CBs are bf16.

multi_tile_128x64: NCRISC stuck at wait_for_brisc_notification. mm_block_init at boot may interfere with dataflow startup. Also cb_scores may be full (16 tiles) from reduce WaitUpfrontNoPop. Need to pop cb_scores after reduce.
Multi-tile hang: NCRISC stuck at wait_for_brisc_notification (never started reader). TRISC2 at reserve_back(cb_scores). Try B_q=1, B_kv=1 to avoid multi-tile block issues and validate the algorithm.
Multi-KV-block hang: reduce helper internally pops the scaler tile, but I also pop manually → double-pop → 2nd reduce hangs waiting for scaler. Fix: remove manual cb_pop_front(cb_scaler) calls.

cb_state: NCRISC at wait_for_brisc_notification, TRISC0 at mm_block_init_short, TRISC2 at reserve_back(cb_scores). Single KV block works, multi KV block hangs.

description: Debug multi-KV-block hang in SDPA
Q block boundary hang: matmul after eltwise (normalize+FillBitcast) needs DataFormatReconfig::INPUT_AND_OUTPUT to reconfigure from eltwise to matmul mode. Try reverting DataFormatReconfig to default.

notes: Single KV block works. Multi KV block hangs. Expert debugger fixed 4 bugs. WaitUpfrontNoPop reduce on cb_scores is the likely root cause of multi-KV-block hang.

hypothesis: Multi-block hang: TRISC0 (unpacker) is at Phase 13 (add<cb_o,cb_scores,cb_o>), while TRISC1 (math) is stuck in _llk_math_matmul_init for Phase 12 (PV matmul), and TRISC2 (packer) is pushing in matmul_block. The three TRISC threads are desynchronized across the matmul→eltwise boundary. The design doc says to call compute_kernel_hw_startup before each matmul→eltwise/reduce transition, but the current kernel only calls it once at boot. NCRISC (reader) is stuck at cb_reserve_back because compute is deadlocked and not consuming tiles.

description: Find compute_kernel_hw_startup definition
Find MatmulBlockShape API

args: hang in scaled_dot_product_attention multi-block compute kernel

observation: Phases 1-11 all complete on KV block (0,0) of Q block 0. Hang is INSIDE Phase 12 PV matmul (matmul_block). P12 start prints, P12 done never prints. This is NOT a multi-block boundary issue - it hangs on the FIRST KV block. The PV matmul MatmulBlockShape::of(1,1,1,2,1,1) has subblock 1x2=2 tiles (fits DEST limit 4). Previous eltwise chain (Phase 11 add) to matmul transition is the suspected cause.

hypothesis: Hang is in Phase 12 PV matmul on the FIRST KV block (0,0). Triage: TRISC1 math at MWDD (waiting for dest release from packer), TRISC2 pack at push_back, TRISC0 unpack already at Phase 13 add. The matmul_block for PV uses TileRowMajor which does reserve_back(row_group_tiles=2) on cb_scores (capacity=2). If the previous Phase 11 add didnt fully drain cb_scores, the reserve_back would block. But cb_scores should be empty after Phase 6 copyP drained it. The real issue may be that TileRowMajor push_back is blocked because cb_scores is full after the matmul pushes 2 tiles and Phase 13 add hasnt started popping yet - but this should just be a normal wait, not a deadlock. Need to investigate if the matmul_block TileRowMajor path has a reserve_back/push_back imbalance.

Trying DataFormatReconfig::NONE on PV matmul since all CBs are bf16 - the reconfig may be causing the pack/unpack format state to be left in an inconsistent state when transitioning from eltwise (Phase 11 add) to matmul (Phase 12 PV). The reconfig calls reconfig_data_format and pack_reconfig_data_format which change SrcA/SrcB and pack formats. If the eltwise chain left the format in a state that the matmul reconfig doesnt expect, it could cause the math thread to hang in init. Since all CBs are bf16, NONE should be safe.

summary: Same hang at P12 after 3 attempts: (1) SubblockMajor, (2) doubled cb_scores capacity, (3) cb_tiles_received print. LLK assert: remaining=128 < num_words=256 on cb_scores push_back. TRISC0 at Phase 13 add, TRISC1 at matmul_init, TRISC2 at push_back. Escalating to static analyzer.

description: Static analysis of SDPA kernels

fix: F1: cb_scores push count mismatch. Phases 1,2,5 push 1 tile each (B_q*B_kv=1). Phase 12 pushes 2 tiles (B_q*D_t=2). The llk_push_tiles asserts remaining>=num_words before push, preventing mid-push wrapping. After 3 single-tile pushes, fifo_wr_ptr is at offset 3, leaving only 1 tile contiguous space but 2 needed. Fix: ensure cb_scores is fully drained (0 tiles) before Phase 12 matmul. The reduce WaitUpfrontNoPop on cb_scores (Phase 3) leaves tiles! Phase 5 in-place expscores Streaming pops/pushes per tile, but Phase 3 reduce leaves B_q*B_kv tiles unpopped. After Phase 5 the tiles from Phase 3 are consumed, but there may be a timing issue. Root cause: the QK^T matmul (Phase 1) pushes 1 tile to cb_scores. transform_in_place (Phase 2) pops/pushes 1 tile in-place. reduce WaitUpfrontNoPop (Phase 3) waits but does NOT pop. So after Phase 3, cb_scores still has 1 tile. Phase 5 eltwise_chain (in-place) reads cb_scores as Streaming, pops 1 tile, pushes 1 tile. After Phase 5, 1 tile. Phase 6 copy pops 1 tile. After Phase 6, 0 tiles. This should be correct...

hypothesis: The root cause is a CB write pointer alignment issue. llk_push_tiles asserts remaining>=num_words BEFORE the push, preventing mid-push wrapping. After Phases 1-6 cycle through 1-tile push/pop operations, fifo_wr_ptr may be at a position where only 1 contiguous page is free before fifo_limit, even though the CB has 0 tiles. The 2-tile push in Phase 12 matmul then fails. Fix: size cb_scores to be large enough that max_push_size always fits contiguously regardless of fifo_wr_ptr position. This means cb_scores_pages >= 2 * max_push_size. Since max_push_size = max(B_q*B_kv, B_q*D_t), we need cb_scores_pages >= 2 * max(B_q*B_kv, B_q*D_t). But we already have that! The issue is that 1-tile operations advance fifo_wr_ptr by 1 each time, and after 3 such operations, fifo_wr_ptr is at 3, leaving 1 page before wrap. Fix: ensure all push counts within a cycle are uniform, or pad the CB to avoid alignment issues.

summary: Hang fixed! All phases 1-14 complete on multiple KV blocks. Root cause: cb_scores CB write-pointer alignment from mixed 1-tile (QK^T scores) and 2-tile (PV output) push patterns. Fix: separate cb_pv_out CB for PV matmul output.

16/29 pass, 13 fail (all PCC). No-mask: long_context_1024=0.988, 2048=0.944, 4096=0.839. Custom mask: all ~0.96. Causal mask: all ~0.97. Two issues: (1) mask application has consistent PCC ~0.96, (2) long-context no-mask accumulates error.

hypothesis: Mask and long-context PCC failures may be caused by Approx::Fast exp approximation. Changing to Approx::Exact for all exp calls (Phases 5, 7, 8) to improve precision. Also, the long-context accumulating error (S=1024: 0.988, S=2048: 0.944, S=4096: 0.839) suggests the online softmax numerics degrade over many KV blocks. The mask PCC ~0.96 is consistent across all shapes, suggesting the -inf mask handling introduces systematic error.

summary: After Approx::Exact: 20/29 pass. All no-mask + long-context pass. All 9 mask tests fail at PCC ~0.96. Mask PCC is consistent regardless of shape, suggesting a systematic issue in the mask application path. Need DEVICE_PRINT to check if -inf mask values propagate correctly through BinaryFpu Add and subsequent exp.

hypothesis: Mask PCC ~0.96 is caused by BinaryFpu<Add> producing score*scale instead of score+mask. DEVICE_PRINT showed scores before mask=4.21875, after mask=0.52734375=4.21875*0.125. The FPU is still in MUL mode from QK^T matmul. The transform_in_place (SFPU MulUnary) leaves math in SFPU mode, and the BinaryFpu<Add> init (add_tiles_init) doesnt fully restore FPU add mode. Fix: replace transform_in_place<MulUnary> with a BinaryFpu<Mul> using cb_scale tile, keeping everything in FPU mode. OR: add an explicit init before the mask BinaryFpu.

Reordering mask before scale didnt change PCC. The BinaryFpu<Add> after QK^T matmul still produces score*0.125 instead of score+mask. The matmul leaves FPU in MUL mode and add_tiles_init doesnt switch it. New approach: scale Q in the reader kernel before the matmul, eliminating the need for transform_in_place entirely. Then BinaryFpu<Add> runs directly after matmul. If it still fails, the issue is in add_tiles_init not restoring FPU mode from matmul MUL to eltwise ADD.

Mask PCC 0.9657 is caused by SFPU exp(-inf) producing NaN instead of 0. The m_i is initialized to -inf (0xFF800000). In Phase 7/8, exp(m_i - m_new) = exp(-inf - finite) = exp(-inf) should be 0, but the SFPU exp approximation may not handle -inf input. Fix: initialize m_i to a large negative finite value (e.g., -1e30) instead of -inf. This ensures exp(large_negative) = 0 without relying on -inf handling.

summary: Timed out after 10min. 140 SUPPORTED cells need to run. Need --dev flag for hang detection.

Hang in PV matmul on shapes with D_t > 4 (D=256,512,1024). The PV matmul MatmulBlockShape::of(1,1,B_q,D_t,B_kv,1) creates subblock of B_q*D_t tiles. With fp32_dest_acc_en=True, DEST limit=4. D_t=8 exceeds this. Need to subblock the PV matmul: use multiple subblocks of max 4 tiles each.
